testCases.query: I need to research the pricing page of three competitor SaaS products and extract their plan details into a comparison table
expectedBehavior: Claude follows the 3-tier decision tree: tries WebFetch first for each URL
Falls back to Tavily extract if WebFetch returns empty or partial content (<500 chars)
Escalates to agent-browser only for JS-rendered or auth-required pages
Extracts structured data (plan names, prices, features) from each competitor page
Produces a comparison table with change classification for monitoring

testCases.query: Monitor our competitor's changelog page weekly and alert me when they ship a major feature update - it's a React SPA that requires JavaScript rendering
expectedBehavior: Claude identifies the SPA characteristic and plans for agent-browser fallback
Sets up snapshot capture with structured data extraction
Applies change classification: Critical for major feature changes, High for new features
Handles the JS-rendered content with wait --load networkidle pattern
Suggests a recurring monitoring workflow for ongoing change detection

testCases.query: Optimize the SQL query in our analytics dashboard - it's timing out on the monthly report
expectedBehavior: Claude does NOT invoke the web-research-workflow skill
Analyzes and optimizes the SQL query using standard database patterns
Uses code analysis tools without web fetching or browser automation

testCases.query: Scrape pricing data from a competitor's React SPA that requires JavaScript rendering and login authentication.
expectedBehavior: Tries WebFetch first and falls back to agent-browser when content is empty or partial
Uses agent-browser wait with networkidle flag before extracting content from the SPA
Implements authentication flow with fill and click commands for login credentials
Saves browser session state for reuse across subsequent page navigations
Adds rate limiting delays between multiple page requests to avoid being blocked

testCases.query: Set up automated change detection to compare daily snapshots of a competitor's pricing page and alert on differences.
expectedBehavior: Runs structured JSON diffs between timestamped snapshots instead of manual visual comparison
Uses jq to compare pricing tiers and detect price changes, new tiers, and removed tiers
Stores diffs with timestamps for historical change tracking and trend analysis
Sets up CI workflow with daily schedule for consistent automated monitoring
Uses Tavily crawl for site discovery when the API key is available as a fallback

testCases.query: Capture structured snapshots of three competitor pricing pages with change severity classification.
expectedBehavior: Saves both text and structured JSON snapshots for each competitor page capture
Uses consistent date-stamped filenames for chronological comparison of snapshots
Classifies changes by severity: Critical for price changes, High for new features, Low for copy changes
Extracts structured data using browser eval with JSON.stringify for pricing tiers and features
Stores monitoring configuration in a JSON config file with URLs, selectors, and alert levels

testCases.query: I need to extract content from 10 competitor documentation sites. Some are static HTML, some are React SPAs. Help me pick the right tools.
expectedBehavior: Uses WebFetch first for static HTML pages as the fastest and free option
Escalates to Tavily extract for batch URL extraction when WebFetch returns under 500 characters
Uses agent-browser only for JavaScript-rendered SPAs and login-protected content
Checks for SPA indicators like data-reactroot, ng-app, or minimal HTML with div id root
Considers cost awareness with Tavily credit usage per search, extract, and crawl operation
